element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Access to improved units and technologies
Military unit expansion and better armor
Maximum unit variety and technological capability

typical_tension_triggers: Resource depletion (trees cut down do not regrow)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Iterative progression with periodic tech gates; tension builds through resource scarcity and competitive age advancement; relief cycles on successful tech unlocks and military production

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction: player starts with hunter-gatherers, focuses on basic resource gathering and town expansion
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate challenge: competitive age advancement, military units introduced, economic efficiency becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: High challenge: Iron Age unlocks full unit roster and technologies; resource scarcity becomes acute; opponent military capabilities peak
notable_spikes: Tool Age transition: introduces military units and inter-civilization tech disparity
Bronze Age transition: religious mechanics and improved armor complexity
Iron Age transition: maximum unit variety and technology combinations available

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player campaigns follow specific historical civilizations (Egyptian, Greek, Babylonian, Yamato) with linear scenario chains and specific victory objectives
Procedurally generated maps with variable resource distribution; enables replayability and competitive balance variance
Four sequential ages (Stone, Tool, Bronze, Iron) act as gates unlocking new unit types, buildings, and technologies; each civilization has unique tech trees
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any campaign scenario with age advancement requirements

notable_level_patterns.description: Finite resources (trees do not regrow); encourages strategic planning and territorial expansion to sustain economy
Player-created scenarios using built-in Scenario Builder tool; enables community-driven level design with variable difficulty and objectives
notable_level_patterns.example_level: User-created custom scenarios (Champa invaders vs. Khmer example noted)
